author: tersec
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/v1.7.0-alpha.8/specs/phase0/p2p-interface.md#maximum-message-sizes

author: potuz
content: Ok so `MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE` is global there and applies to every single message then? But this is only gossip, it's not consensus

author: potuz
content: What prevents me from importing such a Payload for example if I request it by RPC? Or if my user submits to the endpoint?

author: potuz
content: Is this specified as consensus anywhere?

author: jtraglia
content: Where does it say this is only for gossip? I thought this would also apply to req/resp.

author: jtraglia
content: To my knowledge, there is no "max size" limit in consensus. And I don't really think there should be.

author: jtraglia
content: There's an implicit max size from the other limits.

author: tersec
content: if it doesn't come from gossip or req/resp where is it supposed to come from?

author: tersec
content: in terms of this

author: jtraglia
content: Well he does say "Or if my user submits to the endpoint" 😄

author: tersec
content: 7688 removes some of these, except through the custom limits it adds back

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah I'm discussing this with <@881905303011086387> right now actually.

author: jtraglia
content: Those are still just on the networking level though, not consensus.

author: etan_status
content:
https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/v1.7.0-alpha.8/specs/phase0/p2p-interface.md#gossipsub-size-limits
> The size of the uncompressed payload must not exceed MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE or the type-specific SSZ bound, whichever is lower
this applies to all topics

author: etan_status
content:
7688 is a pure hashing change, it does not affect anything else. the limits are exactly the same than before. 
they are just _moved_ from the datatype to a runtime limit that can be freely adjusted without breaking hashing or datatypes.

author: etan_status
content: networking is _part_ of consensus. honest validators MUST implement the networking spec as specified

author: jtraglia
content: No, I don't believe that's what potuz means. "Consensus" is a very specific thing.

author: etan_status
content: maybe he means the state transition function, it's not checked in there, yes

author: jtraglia
content: Like would `process_block` reject the block if something were too big.

author: etan_status
content:
pre-7688 the SSZ type rejects, post-7688 the runtime limit rejects (both as a gossip rule, and inside STF), if exceeded.
the 10 MB MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE is only enforced on the network level, not in the STF

author: etan_status
content: with heze they have these weird rules for the inclusion list where one has to serialize just the transactions part and look at its serialized length (8 KB), that's the only case where I think that the length of _serialized_ data (instead of actual content length) is checked.

author: wemeetagain
content: I suppose the consensus concern is that if a codepath exists to feed size-unchecked data into a chain, you can create a chain split. Whereas pre-7688, sizing was implicitly part of STF bc no size-unchecked data can be represented.

author: wemeetagain
content: In practice, there's only 3 codepaths to ingest chain data: gossip, reqresp, and api. So if we have size checks on all three it should be fine?

author: etan_status
content: it's only a chain split if it is possible to create a valid message for that size

author: etan_status
content: for API there's data from REST (VC / public API publishing blocks through that), and from the engine (kinda trusted, but also historically buggy so CL in practice still wants to validate)

author: etan_status
content: there's also the mechanism where a tiny payload can blow up the beaconblock with deposits by deploying a contract that spends all ETH into the deposit contract, and just sending it a couple thousand ETH without any calldata. where the beaconblock exceeds the 10 MB

author: etan_status
content: essentially, staking pool contract
